source_name: Code4rena Dyad Findings and Analysis Report (2024-04)
finding_title: Design flaw and mismanagement in vault licensing leads to double counting in collateral ratios and positions collateralized entirely with kerosine
summary: Due to a design flaw in the protocol’s management of vault licensing and the deploy script, a significant risk exists where collateral can be counted twice in the calculation of the Collateralization Ratio. This occurs because WETH vaults are incorrectly licensed to both the KeroseneManager and VaultLicenser, allowing users to register the same asset and their NFT ID in both Kerosene vaults and normal vaults. This can allow users to exploit this to greatly inflate their CR calculations, misleading the protocol into considering a position more secure than it truly is, which can prevent necessary liquidations and pose significant risk to the protocol.
root_cause: collateral licensing/accounting split failure allows the same collateral to be counted twice in collateral-ratio and kerosene position calculations
broken_invariants: vault valuation must remain correct under the root cause: collateral licensing/accounting split failure allows the same collateral to be counted twice in collateral-ratio and kerosene position calculations
oracle, TVL, and collateral valuation must use correctly scaled, fresh, and manipulation-resistant inputs
privileged, owner, and license checks must be enforced on every value-moving path, not only on the nominal entry point
attack_preconditions: the affected component is deployed or configured in the way described by the final report
the report-described user, keeper, vault, connector, oracle, or liquidation flow is reachable in the audited code version
attacker_capability: external user, keeper, liquidator, vault participant, or protocol-integrated actor able to trigger the affected flow
trigger_actions: execute the report-described function sequence or state transition
observe mispricing, accounting drift, value loss, fund lock, or denial of service

This occurs because WETH vaults are incorrectly licensed to both the KeroseneManager and VaultLicenser, allowing users to register the same asset and their NFT ID in both Kerosene vaults and normal vaults. why_not_higher: already classified as High in the final C4 report
why_not_lower: included as a validated High/Medium C4 finding with public report evidence and affected code/finding references
common_false_positive_angles: verify the same component and configuration are in scope
check whether the issue is reachable by an untrusted or economically motivated actor
confirm the impact is more than a view-only mismatch, dust loss, or privileged-only misconfiguration
mitigations: The design of the licensing part needs to be re-thinked. The issue here is that the vaults mapping of the KerosineManager contract which is constructed via the method KerosineManager::add is the same mapping that is used by the UnboundedKerosineVault::assetPrice function. You can consider creating two separate mappings. One used only for the price calculation in the UnboundedKerosineVaultassetPrice contract which would only include the classic vaults (weth …); another mapping used for the licensing part which would include the kerosene vaults. Let’s assume these were implemented, we have now two mappings. The DeployV2 should change as follows: kerosineManager.addVaultForOracleCalculation(address(ethVault)); kerosineManager.addVaultForOracleCalculation(address(wstEth)); kerosineManager.add(address(unboundedKerosineVault)); kerosineManager.add(address(boundedKerosineVault)); Assuming the addVaultForOracleCalculation feeds a mapping that will be used by UnboundedKerosineVault::assetPrice while add doesn’t.
